input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a specific plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

## 1. Determine Required Versions

```bash
# Check your .NET SDK version — this dictates workload versions
dotnet --version

# See what MAUI workload version maps to your SDK
dotnet workload search maui-android
```

## 2. Install the MAUI/Android Workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui-android
```

This pulls the Android SDK bindings. The workload manifest pins the exact Android API level and build-tools version needed.

## 3. Install Java (JDK 17)

```bash
apt-get update && apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

## 4. Install the Android SDK (command-line tools only)

```bash
# Download cmdline-tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only for latest)
mkdir -p /usr/local/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
curl -o cmdline-tools.zip https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip
unzip cmdline-tools.zip -d /usr/local/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
mv /usr/local/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools /usr/local/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest

export ANDROID_HOME=/usr/local/android-sdk
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin
```

## 5. Find & Install Correct Android Packages

```bash
# Check which API level your project targets (look in your .csproj):
grep TargetFramework *.csproj
# e.g. net8.0-android34.0 → you need platform 34

# Accept licenses, then install required components
yes | sdkmanager --licenses
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 6. Build

```bash
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -c Release
```

## Key Version Alignment Rules

| Component | How to find correct version |
|---|---|
| Android API level | From `<TargetFramework>` in `.csproj` (e.g., `android34.0` → API 34) |
| Build-tools | Match major version to API level (e.g., `34.x.x`) |
| JDK | .NET 8/9 MAUI requires JDK 17 |
| Workload | Matched automatically by `dotnet workload install` to your SDK band |

## Troubleshooting Tips

- If the build can't find the SDK: set `<AndroidSdkDirectory>` in your `.csproj` or pass `-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$ANDROID_HOME`.
- Run `dotnet workload list` to confirm `maui-android` is installed.
- Missing `zipalign`/`apksigner` → you're missing `build-tools`; re-run `sdkmanager`.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a headless Linux CI server for building a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else. Let me provide a concise, actionable plan.
